summary: Frontend-only UI testing of the redesigned Casa Pâinii (Next.js 16 + Supabase placeholder) app. Verified all 8 requested flows on the preview URL: home + hero carousel, navbar (desktop + mobile menu), events list (search + 5 category filters), event detail, login page, signup page, footer, and HTTP 200 for all public routes. Discovered a critical environment-config bug (Next.js dev server was blocking the preview host as a cross-origin dev resource, so /_next/* client bundles never loaded and React never hydrated, breaking ALL useState/onClick UI: carousel, filters, search, password toggles, mobile menu). Fixed by adding allowedDevOrigins to next.config.ts. After fix, every requested interactive scenario passes.

ui_bugs.issue: data-testid='hero-details-button' and 'hero-join-button' are rendered on all 3 slides simultaneously (all 3 slides stay in the DOM, only opacity/transform differ). Strict-mode Playwright selectors break ('resolved to 3 elements'). Recommend either: (a) suffix the testids with the slide index (e.g. hero-details-button-0/1/2), or (b) render only the active slide, or (c) make non-active slides aria-hidden + pointer-events:none and apply unique testids per slide.

action_items: Please review /app/frontend/next.config.ts — I added allowedDevOrigins for the Emergent preview hostnames (*.preview.emergentagent.com, *.preview.emergentcf.cloud, *.cluster-5.preview.emergentcf.cloud, *.emergentagent.com, *.emergentcf.cloud, *.emergent.host). Without this, Next.js 16 dev server blocks /_next/* requests from the preview URL and the React client bundle never loads → zero hydration → every useState-driven UI element appears dead. Keep this config (or equivalent) for any future Emergent preview-based testing.
(LOW) Disambiguate hero-details-button / hero-join-button testids since all 3 hero slides render at once.

Supabase is still on placeholder keys — login submission, signup submission, /dashboard (correctly redirects to /login), event registration submission, and /live data were intentionally NOT tested end-to-end per the request. User needs to plug real Supabase URL + anon key into /app/frontend/.env before retesting those.
critical_code_review_comments: next.config.ts originally had no allowedDevOrigins — required for Next.js 15+/16 dev server when accessed via a non-localhost preview origin. This is the kind of thing that should be set up once at project scaffolding so subsequent UI work isn't silently broken.

HeroCarousel renders all slides simultaneously and reuses the same data-testid across slides; this works visually but harms testability and accessibility (multiple identical accessible names, non-active CTA links still focusable/clickable beneath the active slide depending on pointer-events).

Auth pages eagerly call createClient() at submit time, which is fine; but with placeholder Supabase keys any submit triggers a network error that the catch surfaces in login-error/signup-error UI. Expected for now.
Server components (dashboard, live) correctly handle missing Supabase keys by redirecting/handling — confirmed they return 200 without runtime crashes.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of requested UI flows after the next.config fix

test_credentials: N/A — Supabase intentionally on placeholder keys; no auth submissions were exercised.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All public routes (/, /events, /events/1, /events/1/register, /login, /signup, /live, /dashboard) return 200 on the preview URL. Client interactivity (HeroCarousel dots, events search + category pills, login/signup password show-hide, mobile navbar) works only because next.config.ts now whitelists Emergent preview hosts via allowedDevOrigins — do NOT remove it. When the user supplies real Supabase keys, retest: (1) login/signup form submission against Supabase, (2) /dashboard rendering for authenticated user, (3) /events/[id]/register submission, (4) /live sermon data. Known minor: duplicated data-testid on hero CTAs across 3 slides causes Playwright strict-mode failures unless you scope by slide.
rca of the issue: Root cause: Next.js 16.2.9 dev server blocks cross-origin requests to /_next/* dev resources by default. The Emergent preview URL (a.cluster-5.preview.emergentcf.cloud / a.preview.emergentagent.com) is treated as cross-origin, so the React client bundle and HMR chunks were refused. SSR HTML rendered fine (hence pages looked correct and returned 200), but no hydration occurred. Symptom: every useState/onClick stopped working — carousel dots inert, events search and category filters had no effect, password show/hide toggles never flipped input type, mobile menu never opened. Reproduction: open any page on the preview URL → check supervisor frontend.err.log → '⚠ Blocked cross-origin request to Next.js dev resource /_next/webpack-hmr from "<preview-host>"'. Fix applied: added allowedDevOrigins to /app/frontend/next.config.ts covering Emergent preview hostname patterns and restarted the frontend supervisor. Verification: re-ran Playwright across home, events, login, signup, mobile — all 6 previously-failing interactions now pass (hero dots switch active state, Tabără filter shows 2 cards, no-match search shows the empty state, both password toggles flip type to 'text', mobile menu becomes visible).
